title: United States and Islamic Republic of Iran resume military attacks after ceasefire collapse
summary: Both sides launch attacks on strategic targets, raising tensions in the Middle East and alarming energy and financial markets.

title: Oil rises more than 6% on breakdown of US-Iran truce
summary: The price of crude oil surges on fears of a supply disruption from the Persian Gulf following the collapse of negotiations.

title: US and European stock markets fall sharply on rising tensions in Iran
summary: The Dow Jones, DAX, and Ibex register significant losses as oil prices rise and geopolitical uncertainty intensifies.

title: Fed minutes show division over pace of rate hikes
summary: Most central bank members support further increases, but persistent inflation and economic slowdown are a concern.

title: IMF and ECB warn of negative economic impact of Iran conflict
summary: The IMF delays growth forecasts until 2027 and the ECB recommends maintaining bank capital buffers.

title: SK Hynix places US IPO with oversubscription of more than seven times
summary: Demand far exceeds supply, allowing the chipmaker to raise nearly $29 billion despite the sector's downturn.

title: UniCredit increases stake in Commerzbank to 48% and opens door to merger
summary: The Italian bank strengthens its position in the German bank, which could trigger a large-scale corporate operation in the European financial sector.

title: Russia bans diesel exports amid severe fuel shortage
summary: Attacks on refineries have reduced production, leading the government to halt exports to ensure domestic supply.

title: Blue Origin raises 1.6 trillion yen and analysts raise SpaceX IPO target
summary: Jeff Bezos' company issues new shares to compete with SpaceX, which could be valued at up to $800 per share in its market debut.

title: Asian stock markets trade lower on geopolitical tensions and tech weakness
summary: Indices in Indonesia, Hong Kong, Australia, China, Taiwan, and Singapore give ground, while the Nikkei also retreats.

title: Broadcom secures $30 billion deal with Apple until 2031
summary: The semiconductor company consolidates its position as a strategic iPhone supplier, although its shares fell 15% in June.

title: China's Zhipu AI raises $4 billion in a capital increase in Hong Kong
summary: The artificial intelligence startup sells shares at a discount to finance its growth in the competitive AI sector.

title: US consortium extends exclusivity for $5.3 billion purchase of Steadfast
summary: The Australian insurer receives an improved offer and extends the deadline while negotiating the definitive terms of the acquisition.

title: Kioxia shares surge on stock exchange without any corporate announcement to explain it
summary: The Japanese NAND memory manufacturer rises sharply amid a volatile chip market.

title: South Korea enters bear market on semiconductor sector pessimism
summary: The South Korean stock index falls more than 20% from its recent high, dragged down by weak outlook for chipmakers.

title: Nest pension fund to invest £1 billion in venture capital
summary: The initiative aims to improve returns for British savers through investments in startups and technology.

title: Rare earths mined in US mines linked to Trump sold to Asian buyers
summary: Despite tariffs, the strategic material ends up in Asia, casting doubt on the US mining independence policy.

title: London records worst IPO drought in years
summary: Companies opt to list their shares on other markets, weakening the City's role as a global financial hub.

title: Paramount delays closing of Warner Bros merger amid possible state lawsuit
summary: Oregon's investigation and the threat of legal action by several states force the deal to be postponed until late July.

title: FCA accuses lawyer of insider trading in Seraphine sale
summary: The British regulator files charges for insider trading in the transaction of the fashion firm Seraphine.
